food_subgroup: Noodle dishes with mixed protein and vegetable toppings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Indomie-based restaurant noodle dishes with mixed toppings. FOODON_00002187 (noodle dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Noodles, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Indomie-based mixed-topping noodle dishes in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific restaurant noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of jumbo Indomie noodles with toppings is approximately 400 g, comprising a large portion of dry-tossed noodles with hotdog slices, carrot strips, squid rings, and a melted cheese slice.

nutrient_source.title: Fried noodles (mee goreng) and instant noodle component data; USDA FoodData Central entries for hotdog, carrot, squid, and processed cheese
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: Indomie instant noodles (dry-tossed, ~55% of dish weight) anchored to HPB Foodo instant noodle data; hotdog (~10%), carrot (~10%), squid rings (~15%), and cheese slice (~10%) anchored to USDA FoodData Central entries (FDC IDs: 172949 frankfurter, 170393 raw carrot, 175159 squid cooked, 173414 American cheese). Values represent a blended per-100g estimate for the composite dish as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — instant noodles analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific restaurant dish. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (boiled/drained, GI ~52, low category), which constitutes the dominant carbohydrate source (~55% by weight). The presence of protein (hotdog, squid), fat (cheese, hotdog), and fibre (carrot) from other components is expected to moderately lower the effective GI relative to plain noodles. GI 52 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate for the composite dish. GL per serving = (52 × 82.0 g available carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 42.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2080 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from instant noodle seasoning, processed hotdog, and cheese, which can substantially exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) with high glycemic load per serving (~42.6) due to large noodle portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI of this dish is in the low range, the large serving size results in a high glycemic load per meal; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a reduced portion size and pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, saturated fat from processed cheese and hotdog, and trans fat from instant noodle processing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish carries a high cardiovascular risk profile due to its combination of elevated sodium (~2080 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed cheese and hotdog, and trace trans fats from instant noodle manufacturing; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish only occasionally and in reduced portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jumbo Indomie Noodles with Hotdog, Carrot, Squid and Cheese is a popular Singaporean hawker-style and casual restaurant dish served at establishments such as The Noodle Place. The dish features a large portion of Indomie instant noodles prepared dry (goreng-style), tossed with the signature seasoning sachet and served with sliced hotdog, julienned or sliced fresh carrot, tender battered or plain squid rings, and a melted processed cheese slice on top. The noodles are boiled, drained, and then dry-tossed with the seasoning, giving them a slightly chewy texture with a savory, mildly spicy flavour profile characteristic of Indomie goreng. The hotdog contributes a smoky, salty note; the carrot adds a mild sweetness and crunch; the squid rings provide a chewy seafood element; and the cheese slice melts into the hot noodles, adding creaminess and richness. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (~178 kcal per 100 g, approximately 712 kcal per serving) with moderate protein (~8.2 g per 100 g) from the squid, hotdog, and cheese, and a significant carbohydrate load (~20.5 g per 100 g) from the noodles. Sodium content is high (~520 mg per 100 g), making this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), but the large serving size produces a high glycemic load, requiring portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten, dairy, soy, or shellfish allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or USDA entry exists for this specific restaurant composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using HPB Foodo instant noodle data and USDA FoodData Central entries for individual ingredients. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for instant noodles. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this estimated composite dish. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. All other -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source set rather than confirmed zero values. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant jumbo noodle portions in Singapore. GI of 52 is a conservative analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database instant noodle entry; the actual composite GI may be lower due to protein, fat, and fibre contributions from toppings. Halal status is listed as false because hotdog and squid sourcing cannot be confirmed as halal-certified from available data; consumers should verify with the restaurant directly. This dish is sold at The Noodle Place - Circular Road via field survey data at SGD 8.90.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced but output was empty/invalid JSON (validator reported invalid JSON at char 0).
Iteration 2: Second draft produced but again resulted in invalid JSON per validator feedback.

Iteration 3: Complete rebuild of JSON from scratch with strict valid JSON output, no markdown fences, no commentary. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria verified internally before output. Aliases consistency check performed: all aliases_by_language strings confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array. All -1 sentinel values documented in notes. GI category verified: 52 ≤ 55 = low. Ontology match_type values restricted to allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none). Zero-macro justification not required as no core macro is zero.
